text.text:
Tried Nana Green Tea Pasadena since they just opened 🍵
Place was super crowded and everyone had to order from the machine, so it was kinda hectic at first 😭 They told us most food was sold out already, so we just got matcha drinks first and honestly they were really good. Not too sweet and the matcha taste was strong in a good way.
Later some food became available again so we got the curry pork katsu, really remind me of the one I had in Japan. Portion was good too.
Only thing I didnt really like was the hojicha cake, but overall def will come back ✨

text.text:
Visited on a Sunday afternoon and the line was not too bad and moved quickly. There are two options to order: one on a tablet and one with a cashier.

I ordered the chicken Katsu curry and the soft serve matcha latte. Both were very tasty. I wasn't expecting to get miso soup with my meal, but it was a welcomed surprise.

Overall, would come again. I would recommend parking at the structure on Fairoaks/Raymond due to the higher rates for the parking structure closer to this establishment.

text.text:
Highly anticipated place originated from Tokyo specializing in Japanese desserts, think of parfaits with different toppings and Japanese comfort food like katsu, curry bowls, salmon sashimi bowl. The desserts have highlights of warabimochi, flavors like matcha/sesame/hojicha jellies/ice cream. They have serve softs too. Definitely a heaven for those who love matcha/hojicha/sesame taste food! Staff is professional and friendly. I love that if you don't pick up your order, they will put frozen items like parfait, ice cream etc in the fridge so the quality won't be affected. The sweet level for desserts is according to the Japanese level so not too sweet.
Matcha parfait is perfectly sweetened matcha has slight bitterness and mochi was so smooth freshly made.

Plus, the dessert place that has amazing food?! Hello? The food definitely compares to the level of Japanese casual chain.
Highly recommend Sukiyaki and chicken karaage curry.

Update: only minus is parking situation!! Located in Pasadena downtown parking is $12 the whole day?? If you only there for this specific thing, it may hurt your wallet. But this place is bomb.com

text.text:
We’ve been waiting for Nana’s Green Tea to open somewhere close to us in SoCal for years and it finally did today! After visiting and enjoying Nana’s Green Tea in Tokyo and Seattle a couple of times over the years, we were super excited to learn about the grand opening of the Pasadena location! We knew there would be a long line today since it was the much anticipated grand opening but we decided to go anyways because we were craving their special parfaits.

We came on opening day at around 3:30pm and after waiting behind probably 40 people (surprisingly much less than what the morning brought), we finally reached the front of the line that snaked around the first corner of the building. The wait wasn’t too bad since we were in the shade, which was nice.

Instead of ordering at the counter with workers, you just order what you want from a kiosk screen and grab a table that is available. The inside was pretty spacious and well lit with natural lighting from the big windows. There with lots of tables for 2-4 people and some long window-side bar-type tables in the front of the restaurant for those eating alone.

We ordered the salmon sashimi don with a soft boiled egg and miso soup, chicken karaage (5 pieces), and both a Matcha Mochi Parfait and a Hojicha Mochi Parfait (we doubled up on dessert because we had waited so long in line and felt like the purchases had to be worth it). We felt like things were pretty well controlled and organized and eventually our food was ready for pick up at the counter.

The salmon sashimi don was good and hit the spot. Nothing special to be honest but good. The dish had salmon sashimi on rice with pickled ginger, with some wasabi and my add-on soft boiled egg. There was also an accompanying miso soup which was good. The karaage was good and also nothing unique. It came with a spring mix and lemon. No sauces.

But the real highlight of the visit were the parfaits! Just as delicious as we remember them being in Tokyo! The perfect dessert for a warm spring day. Can’t imagine how refreshing it’ll be on hot summer days. I personally enjoyed the Matcha Parfait better but both were great! Similar stuff in both except for the taste of the ice cream and jelly/pudding cubes. Includes vanilla ice cream, mochi, red bean paste, and essentially corn flakes with whipped cream on top. Delicious and definitely the highlight from any visit to Nana’s Green Tea!

After finishing, you’re supposed to bus your own table before you leave.

Overall, happy to finally have Nana’s Green Tea closeby and happy to be able to have one of their parfaits without having to fly across the Pacific! We felt that despite opening day’s long lines, things were well run, organized, and pretty quick overall. Will definitely be back once its new hype dies down a little bit. But Nana’s will definitely be one of our local dessert spots.

Lastly, the location is close to a bunch of other places in Pasadena (good restaurants and bakeries, a nearby park). There is paid street parking until really late at night. But if you park far away enough, there’ll be free parking spots as well. It might just take a little driving around.

Will definitely be back this summer!

text.text: Its a hot day so I ordered a hojicha warabimochi parfait. Its bigger than I expected. Not only that I also wanted a "small" appetizer to have with a sweet treat. The potato salad is by no means small, its a good portion for 2 people. Im amazed by portion for the good price they offer and the food is amazing. I will definitely come again but at an earlier time since it is quite busy due to the heat wave.
